name: APA 7th Edition (Psychology/Social Sciences)
notes: APA 7th edition format. Uses apa7 document class. Requires apacite for references. Running head required. Level headings: centered bold (1), left-aligned bold (2), indented bold italic (3). Tables and figures at end or inline. Include keywords after abstract.
writing_guide:
TONE: Formal, precise, objective. Use past tense for results and procedure ('Participants completed...', 'Results indicated...'). Use present tense only for established facts and conclusions. Prefer active voice per APA 7th guidelines. Use 'we' for multiple authors, 'I' for single author. Avoid anthropomorphism ('The study found...' is wrong; 'We found...' is correct).

ABSTRACT: 150-250 words. Structured: objective, method (sample, design), results (effect sizes and CIs), conclusions. Include keywords line after abstract. Register the study if applicable and note registration number.

STRUCTURE: Use APA heading levels strictly. Introduction has no heading (it follows the title). Literature Review synthesizes themes, not paper-by-paper summaries. Method section must include: Participants (demographics, N, power analysis), Materials/Measures (reliability coefficients), Procedure, Data Analysis Plan. Results: report effect sizes (Cohen's d, eta-squared) and confidence intervals for EVERY statistical test, not just p-values. Discussion: summarize findings, compare to literature, state limitations, suggest future directions.

CITATIONS: 40-60 references typical. Use (Author, Year) format. Cite seminal works and recent empirical studies. Every claim in Introduction and Discussion needs a citation.

FIGURES/TABLES: APA-formatted tables (no vertical lines, minimal horizontal lines). Report exact statistics in tables (M, SD, t, p, d, 95% CI). Place figures/tables after References or inline per journal preference.

REVIEWER EXPECTATIONS: Power analysis justifying sample size. Effect sizes everywhere. Pre-registration or explanation of why not. Ethical approval (IRB) stated. Measures' psychometric properties reported.

DO NOT: Report only p-values without effect sizes. Do not use causal language for correlational designs. Do not skip power analysis. Do not use Level 1 headings for Introduction. Do not anthropomorphize results.
